item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still would be bad. St. Bernards are certainly much more dangerous than chihuahuas, but they're still not the dominant species of the planet, which is what Amaroq intended in the first place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think having another human bring a St. Bernard to him would still be worth a try.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm curious to see how this thing would react to a wolfhound, or maybe a pitbull.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So I think the Foundation would NEVER let the doctor bring such dogs to 3951. Like damn, he's all like "you did WHAT to dogs?!!?!" And you think it's a good idea to bring a chihuahua in front of him. Like, i woulda brought like 5+ bigass hunting dogs and be as respectable as i could. I would literally bring wolves if he wouldn't have realized I'd be lying.

I'd say punishment to whoever decided this is warrented, but then, I guess that's what the first paragraphs are implying… He was probably killed after the interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe I missed it, but why is SCP-2871 mentioned?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this god more than I love anyone else currently living. +1

I like this, along with the whole dormant thing, in a ancient anomalous manner. I was actually starting to get worried near the end! Thought the doctor was gonna get consumed! But no, relief. Also, is it possible SCP-3951 is the reason SCP-2457 are wolfs? Like the connection feel.

item.title: (no title)
item.wikidot:authorName: J E S T E R
item.content:encoded:
All those Good Boys are actually God Boys? hm…
+1 for "I never should have given you idiots thumbs."

One slight thing though: In the first interview's information blurb at the top, the doc's name is Dr. Arnatsiaq, when every other time (including during the actual interview) it's Dr. Aglukark. Otherwise, great job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ehh, not feeling this one. The long dog concept immediately brings to mind long corg, but whereas that one was charmingly out of the box this is just some over the top dialogue. I mean hell, we’re supposed to buy that a diety who’s been isolated for at least 10,000 years just happens to talk like a sarcastic millennial? There’s also some discrepancies in the number and in the doctor’s name that need to be fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The twist here is meant to be that - holy shit, we're not made in God's image. We've made God's chosen race into our pets.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Psst, you have a couple of "SCP-2871" still in there.

Edit: I'm not really a fan of what's going on here. I think the concept of "Diety SCP's" is rather overplayed at this point, and I think the writing, especially in the dialogue, isn't very interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I thought the writing was solid, the concept leaves something to be desired. A big old wolf is cool, but it needs some kind of twist to grab you. Making it god is certainly an interesting idea, but I don't feel it's the right one for this particular dude. There's a million billion strange traits that you could give a wolf. I did like the quick transition between the super formal, mystical language to just regular modern human-ish speech.

item.title: Canine God 2: Electric Boogaloo
item.content:encoded: Well, it's up! Again! Thanks to not_a_seagull, CaptainKirby, Demonhorde, and zzuxon_temp for crit!
